name: Chatbot-Generated Chemistry Responses Expanded Training Heterogeneity
statement: Martin and Graulich compared additional real student data, chatbot paraphrases, and chatbot-generated responses to address the difficulty of gathering diverse chemistry reasoning data; combinations including chatbot responses yielded the best machine-human score agreements.
population_or_context: Undergraduate chemistry reasoning about reaction mechanisms.
quantitative_note: The abstract reports significantly improved ML model performance and best machine-human agreements when authentic and/or paraphrased responses were combined with chatbot responses.
evaluation_method: Machine-human score agreement across validation conditions.
sparsity_or_imbalance_motivation: Resource constraints limited collection of diverse training data capturing nuances of student reasoning.
synthetic_data_research_role: Expanded training-data size and heterogeneity and enabled a more detailed categorization of causal reasoning.
reported_metrics: Machine-human score agreement; detailed metrics require full-text follow-up.
limitations: This baseline extraction uses the open abstract rather than full method tables.
Model and prompt details need follow-up extraction before finer claims.
